top_signals.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $60,000 as Strategy Selloff Refuels Anxiety
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin tumbled more than 3% toward $58,000 on Tuesday as investors reversed an initial vote of confidence in Michael Saylor’s financing overhaul at Strategy Inc., raising fresh concerns that one of the cryptocurrency’s biggest buyers may no longer be a consistent source of demand.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: US and Iran negotiators head to Doha, but meeting uncertain - Reuters
top_signals.summary: US and Iran negotiators head to Doha, but meeting uncertain  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Crypto companies have spent $189M so far on 2026 US election cycle: Report
top_signals.summary: The crypto industry, big tech and gambling interest groups are betting big on politicians in the 2026 primaries and elections, with almost $300 million shelled out so far.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: The real AI risk isn’t job losses, it’s who can see what
top_signals.summary: Beyond the fearmongering, it’s a more mundane AI threat that will be making waves in boardrooms, writes Paul Armstrong.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: US racks up about 400 wins over illegal World Cup streaming sites
top_signals.summary: The World Cup’s organizing body, FIFA, helped identify hundreds of  domains taken down in an action organized by the U.S., along with the help of U.S. broadcaster NBC Universal and other entities.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
